description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.json_schema

Closes **F17** (HIGH, Performance). No parallelism, no `:telemetry`, no streaming/progress output; full report assembled in memory.

**Dependencies**: requires .18 (Task.Supervisor exists) before parallelism can land.

## Deliverable
- `Task.Supervisor.async_stream_nolink/4` for the mutation loop with `max_concurrency: System.schedulers_online()`, configurable via `--max-concurrency`
- `:telemetry` events at well-defined points: enumeration_done, baseline_done, coverage_done, site_started, site_completed, run_completed
- Streaming NDJSON output to stdout (or `--json` file) when `--stream` is set; per-site result emitted as the site completes
- Progress feedback to stderr (default on TTY; suppressed with `--no-progress`)
- Report still aggregable to the final JSON shape (existing in-memory mode preserved as default)
- Revise json_schema.spec.md for NDJSON variant; mutation_pipeline.spec.md for parallelism + telemetry contract.

## Allowed touches
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (parallelism)
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (streaming + NDJSON)
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex (flags)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (progress + telemetry wiring)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md, json_schema.spec.md
- Corresponding test files
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope
- Telemetry-poller / observable backend wiring — events are emitted; consumers configure their own subscribers
- ETS / DETS-backed result store (in-memory is fine for v1.1)

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] Parallel run produces byte-identical JSON to serial run on a deterministic scope (concurrency must not break r2)
- [ ] NDJSON variant validates against json_schema.spec.md
- [ ] Telemetry events fire (test with handler)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

- .spec/specs/json_schema.spec.md (new r10, r11 + v5 stub + surface)
- README.md (Parallel mode and observability section; new flag table rows)
- CHANGELOG.md (Added entry; cites F17)

Tests added (25 new tests, all green):
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_parallel_test.exs (6 tests — byte-identical determinism, input-order collection, telemetry site events, on_site_completed callback ordering)
- test/mutagen_ex/json_streamer_test.exs (8 tests — start/result/compile_error/end envelopes, line-delimited stream)
- test/mutagen_ex/progress_test.exs (4 tests — enabled?/1, report/2 padding/output)
- test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs (7 new tests — --max-concurrency/--stream/--no-progress flag parsing, :invalid_max_concurrency error path)

Verification stubs flipped to execute: true:
- mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v7 (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_parallel_test.exs)
- mutagen.json_schema.v5 (mix test test/mutagen_ex/json_streamer_test.exs)

Merge gate status:
- [x] mix test green: 456 tests, 0 failures
- [x] Parallel run produces byte-identical JSON to serial run on a deterministic scope: verified by `parallel and serial runs produce byte-equal results on deterministic input` in mutation_runner_parallel_test.exs (uses Agent-backed ExUnitFake for determinism; results, compile_errors, state_drift_warning, warnings all assert byte-equal across max_concurrency: 1 vs 4)
- [x] NDJSON variant validates against json_schema.spec.md: r10/r11 added; v5 stub exercises wire-shape, kind-discriminator, line-delimited stream; 8/8 tests green
- [x] Telemetry events fire (test with handler): `:telemetry.attach_many` in mutation_runner_parallel_test verifies start+stop counts and metadata shape; e2e_slow tests passing exercise the production handler attach path

Summary: Adds the parallel-dispatch / telemetry / NDJSON-streaming / progress-feedback infrastructure called out by F17. The mutation runner now dispatches per-site tasks through Task.Supervisor.async_stream_nolink/4 under MutagenEx.TaskSup with :ordered: true; results fold sequentially over the input-ordered stream so taint/warnings/counters are deterministic across max_concurrency values. The Mix-task default for --max-concurrency is 1 (v1.0-equivalent) because the in-process pipeline shares ExUnit's global server and the Code.Server's per-module load locks across tasks — explicit --max-concurrency N is the opt-in path for callers with collision-free input. Telemetry events (`run.start/stop`, `coverage.start/stop`, `baseline.start/stop`, `enumeration.stop`, `site.start/stop`) fire via `MutagenEx.Telemetry`; NDJSON streaming via `MutagenEx.JsonStreamer` produces lines byte-equal to the aggregate document; progress feedback via `MutagenEx.Progress` auto-detects TTY on stderr.

Note: `test/mutagen_ex/golden/end_to_end_scenario_4_infinite_looper.json.actual` was already present (untracked) on the epic worktree before this ticket — pre-existing line-drift in that golden, not caused by mutagen-wrd.30. Not staged or committed.

title: Parallel mutation loop + telemetry + NDJSON streaming + progress feedback
